plain: a shell command Kimi runs at a lifecycle moment; it gets the event JSON on stdin and can only ALLOW or BLOCK
why: hansard's whole doorman rides on hooks — but Kimi's are a gate, not a context channel

plain: Kimi acts on a hook ONLY via action=block (exit 2, or JSON permissionDecision:deny); it ignores any context the hook prints
why: this is the load-bearing constraint — coach/compass/escalate have no native path

plain: the JSON a hook prints to make Kimi refuse a tool call
why: the one output field Kimi parses (runner.py:78); hansard's reject channel

plain: on Claude/Codex, text a hook injects into the model silently without blocking
why: Kimi has NO equivalent — kimisoul.py reads only action+reason; this is what breaks the soft channels

plain: Kimi's names for the bash / write-file / edit-file tools
why: the PreToolUse matcher and the tool-input shim target these instead of Claude's Bash/Write/Edit

plain: a SKILL.md instruction file Kimi loads from extra_skill_dirs, callable as /skill:<name>
why: how the 5 hansard slash commands port (no native /command system)
